description: Extensible rule definitions for all 7 Stage Validation Agents. Users can add custom rules to the custom_rules array.

disposition_rules.BLOCK: Any CRITICAL finding that fails triggers BLOCK disposition
disposition_rules.ADVISORY: Any HIGH finding that fails triggers ADVISORY (unless already BLOCK)
disposition_rules.PASS: All findings pass, or only MEDIUM/LOW failures

rules.name: Flattening Completeness
rules.description: Every file in original/ has a corresponding .md in flattened/. Cross-reference source-manifest.json with flatten-results.json.

rules.name: Flattening Quality
rules.description: Char count vs original file size ratio is reasonable (>10 chars/KB for PDF, >50 chars/KB for DOCX). No truncation markers. Table structures preserved for XLSX.

rules.name: Domain Detection Confidence
rules.description: domain-context.json passes EITHER (clear_winner AND confidence>=0.5) OR (margin>=30 AND top_score>=60). Strong-margin gate added 2026-05-20 to mirror phase1.5-domain-win.md Step 5 — with 6 domains splitting the score pool, share-of-total confidence rarely reaches 0.8 even for overwhelming winners.

lessons_learned.finding: MARS Phase 1.5 selected state_local_government correctly (margin=125, top_score=242) but confidence=0.39 failed the 0.8 share-of-total gate. The rule blocked Stage 1 progression despite the phase output being correct.
lessons_learned.detail: Share-of-total math (top_score / sum_of_all_scores) is fundamentally bounded by 1/N for N domains — with 6 domains, even a runaway winner with 70% of the points only hits confidence=0.7. The right signal is margin-over-second-place, which the phase file already uses.
lessons_learned.guidance: Rule logic now applies both gates and passes if EITHER fires. Confidence path retained for legacy compatibility; strong-margin path catches dominant winners.

rules.name: Evaluation Criteria Extraction
rules.description: EVALUATION_CRITERIA.json has at least 3 evaluation factors. Weights sum to ~100%. At least 1 factor has explicit (non-inferred) weight.

lessons_learned.finding: False positive on simple RFQs with Quality-Based Selection (QBS)
lessons_learned.detail: Simple RFQs may legitimately have only 2 evaluation factors (e.g., Company Qualifications 60%, Key Personnel 40%). The minimum-3 threshold should apply only to complex RFPs.
lessons_learned.guidance: QBS/RFQ solicitations with exactly 2 factors where weights sum to 100% should score as PASS, not FAIL. Check solicitation type before applying the >= 3 threshold.

rules.name: Compliance Extraction Depth
rules.description: Ratio of mandatory items in COMPLIANCE_MATRIX.json to SHALL/MUST occurrences in flattened text > 0.7.

lessons_learned.finding: Items/keywords ratio misleading due to contextual SHALL/MUST keywords
lessons_learned.detail: SHALL/MUST keywords are often contextual — appearing in section headers, quoted regulations, and compound sentences restating the same obligation. Initial ratio of 0.58 improved to 0.745 after keyword mapping analysis revealed duplication.
lessons_learned.guidance: Rule should consider keyword deduplication before computing the ratio. Many SHALL/MUST occurrences are continuations, headers, or regulatory quotes, not distinct mandatory items. Deduplicate by clause before comparing to COMPLIANCE_MATRIX.json item count.

lessons_learned.finding: Lessons_learned guidance from 2026-03-03 was captured but never wired into the rule
lessons_learned.detail: MARS run produced ratio=0.493 (760 items vs 1543 raw keywords) and blocked Stage 1. After implementing clause-level dedup per the original guidance, denominator drops dramatically (target ~760-850 distinct clauses) and the ratio normalizes.
lessons_learned.guidance: Rule logic now applies sentence-level dedup + filters for section headers, regulatory blockquotes, and definition lists. Both raw_count and clause_dedup_count are reported in details for diagnostic visibility.

rules.name: Compliance Category Distribution
rules.description: Mandatory items span at least 3 different categories.

rules.name: Jurisdiction Consistency Across Stage 1
rules.description: domain-context.json jurisdiction_anchor.state must match the jurisdiction observed in EVALUATION_CRITERIA.json, COMPLIANCE_MATRIX.json, and SUBMISSION_STRUCTURE.json. Catches Phase 1.5 hallucination where a fabricated state name (e.g., Alaska) propagates into downstream phases that read the actual RFP correctly (e.g., Oregon).

lessons_learned.finding: Phase 1.5 hallucinated 'State of Alaska' for State of Oregon MARS RFP
lessons_learned.detail: When domain scoring landed on 'default' (gov 117 vs finance 100, below clear-winner margin), the agent compensated by writing an ad-hoc 'mars_domain_hints' block with an invented State of Alaska / Title 36-37 attribution. Phase 1.6 cited 'Oregon ORS 279B' and Phase 1.7 cited Oregon Secretary of State — both read the RFP correctly. A cross-stage check would have caught this immediately.
lessons_learned.guidance: Compare jurisdiction tokens across all four Stage 1 outputs. Any disagreement is a CRITICAL block that re-runs Phase 1.5 with the disagreeing-token evidence appended to the prompt.

rules.name: Requirement Source Coverage
rules.description: Every flattened RFP document section containing SHALL/MUST language has at least one requirement traced to it.

rules.name: Deduplication Quality
rules.description: Reduction ratio (raw to normalized) between 10-40%. Below 10% = duplicates remain. Above 40% = over-merged.

rules.name: Priority Distribution
rules.description: CRITICAL 5-15%, HIGH 20-35%, MEDIUM 35-55%, LOW 10-25%.

rules.name: Category Coverage
rules.description: Requirements span at least 5 categories. Each compliance framework from domain-context.json has at least 1 requirement.

rules.name: Compliance-to-Requirement Mapping
rules.description: Every mandatory item in COMPLIANCE_MATRIX.json maps to at least one requirement in requirements-normalized.json.

rules.name: Workflow-Requirement Alignment Quality
rules.description: For MEDIUM quality matches (0.6-0.8 similarity), verify semantic alignment.

rules.name: Sample Data Integration
rules.description: Data entities from sample-data-analysis.json appear in requirements.

rules.name: Strategy Readiness Check
rules.description: Minimum: 50+ requirements, 3+ categories, evaluation criteria extracted, compliance gate passed.

rules.name: Deliverable Table Row Cap Check
rules.description: No _Showing N of M_ row-cap notice appears in any outputs/*.md file. No empty mitigation cell (|  |) appears in a risk-priority row. Catches row-cap truncation and empty-mitigation bugs visible in evaluator PDFs.
rules.incident: 2026-05-18: _Showing 15 of 281 risks_ and _Showing 200 of 762_ visible in PDFs; empty | | mitigation column in every risk row

rules.name: Specification Requirement Coverage
rules.description: Every CRITICAL and HIGH priority requirement is addressed by at least one specification document.

lessons_learned.finding: Coverage threshold doesn't exclude administrative/procurement requirements
lessons_learned.detail: Administrative requirements (form submission, bid packaging, vendor disclosures, insurance paperwork) are legitimately NOT addressed in technical specifications — they belong in bid response sections (Phase 8). Including them in the denominator artificially deflates coverage from ~97% to 87.6%.
lessons_learned.guidance: Coverage calculation should exclude requirements with category=ADM and type=COMPLIANCE that relate to procurement process. Alternatively, lower the effective threshold to 90% with an explicit note about administrative exclusion. Technical coverage should be evaluated independently from procurement compliance.

rules.name: Specification Internal Consistency
rules.description: Technology choices align across specs (architecture, security, interop).

rules.name: Domain Alignment
rules.description: Specs reference domain-appropriate standards and technologies.

rules.name: Risk-to-Specification Coverage
rules.description: Every HIGH severity risk mitigation reflected in at least one spec.

rules.name: Entity Definition Completeness
rules.description: Every data entity from workflow requirements has a definition in ENTITY_DEFINITIONS.md.

lessons_learned.finding: Entity candidate extraction includes non-system entities
lessons_learned.detail: Entity candidates extracted from requirements include administrative and legal form types (General Partnership, Sole Proprietorship, Interlocal Agreement) that are legal/organizational concepts, not system data entities. These inflate the candidate count and deflate the completeness ratio.
lessons_learned.guidance: Entity candidate extraction should filter to data entities only — objects that will be stored, queried, or processed by the system. Exclude legal/organizational form types, procurement concepts, and regulatory classification terms that appear as nouns in requirements but are not system-managed entities.

rules.name: Specification Depth Ratio
rules.description: Spec size per requirement addressed is proportional. Minimum 200 bytes per requirement.

rules.name: Tech Stack Version Consistency (Bid Markdown)
rules.description: Every technology version cited in outputs/*.md and outputs/bid-sections/*.md must match shared/tech-lifecycle-evidence.json recommended_version. Catches stale .NET 8 / .NET 9 text after a .NET 10 migration. Exempt: text inside negative-citation or ADR-alternatives context (competitor warnings, rejected-version rationale).
rules.incident: 2026-05-18: .NET 8 LTS / .NET 9 LTS stale text found in 03_TECHNICAL.md and RISK_REGISTER.md while SVAs reported PASS 94.2/100

rules.name: Bidirectional Traceability Completeness
rules.description: Forward: every req in UNIFIED_RTM.json has spec links. Backward: every spec has requirements mapped.

rules.name: Traceability Quality Audit
rules.description: Sample 20% of mappings and verify semantic alignment. Flag likely false positives.

rules.name: Red Team Evaluator Simulation
rules.description: Simulate evaluation using EVALUATION_CRITERIA.json weights. Produce mock score breakdown.

rules.name: Compliance Forward Traceability
rules.description: Every mandatory item traces through: item -> requirement -> specification. Any break = potential disqualification.

rules.name: Estimation Consistency
rules.description: Effort per requirement is reasonable. Total between 500-100,000 hours (configurable via threshold_max).

rules.name: Estimation Risk Alignment
rules.description: High-risk requirements should have higher effort estimates.

rules.name: Manifest Accuracy
rules.description: Every file in MANIFEST.md exists with stated size. No missing files.

rules.name: Executive Summary Statistics
rules.description: Statistics in EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md match actual data in shared JSON files.

rules.name: Navigation Guide Link Validity
rules.description: Every file referenced in NAVIGATION_GUIDE.md exists.

rules.name: Context Bundle Completeness
rules.description: bid-context-bundle.json aggregates 10+ source files. Critical sections populated.

rules.name: Persona Coverage Minimum
rules.description: PERSONA_COVERAGE.json overall_score >= 80. Each persona coverage_score >= 70.

rules.name: Win Probability Threshold
rules.description: WIN_SCORECARD.json win_probability >= 60%. Advisory only.

rules.name: Gap Analysis Review
rules.description: GAP_ANALYSIS.md has no HIGH severity gaps remaining.

rules.name: Risk Mitigation Completeness
rules.description: Every HIGH/CRITICAL risk has: mitigation strategy, owner_role, verification_criteria.

rules.name: Bid Context Integrity
rules.description: bid-context-bundle.json counts match source JSON files. No stale data.

rules.name: Evaluation Weight Coverage
rules.description: Each evaluation factor weighted >= 20% has at least one spec and one requirements category addressing it.

rules.name: Win Theme Semantic Threading
rules.description: Each win theme appears in context with evidence, not just as headings. 2+ substantive paragraphs per bid section.

rules.name: Risk-to-Bid Traceability
rules.description: Every HIGH risk mitigation from UNIFIED_RTM.json verified present in bid text.

rules.name: Compliance-to-Bid Verification
rules.description: Every mandatory item from COMPLIANCE_MATRIX.json addressed in bid. Full chain verified.

rules.name: Cross-Document ID Integrity
rules.description: All requirement/risk IDs referenced in bid sections exist in source JSON files. No phantom references.

rules.name: Evaluator Persona Satisfaction
rules.description: Each evaluator persona's top 3 concerns are addressed in bid.

rules.name: Format Compliance
rules.description: Page count estimate within page limits. Font/margin requirements met.

rules.name: Statistic Consistency
rules.description: Numbers cited in bid match source data (requirement count, coverage percentage, etc.).

rules.name: Competitive Positioning Presence
rules.description: Solution includes competitive contrast. Incumbent weaknesses from CLIENT_INTELLIGENCE.json referenced.

rules.name: Diagram Legibility
rules.description: Every rendered diagram PNG in outputs/bid/ meets font-size threshold (>=14pt at print scale) OR its mermaid source declares fontSize >= 14px in the theme init directive.

rules.name: Diagram Color Contrast (WCAG AA)
rules.description: Every classDef color pair declared in mermaid blueprints (fill vs color) meets WCAG 2.2 AA contrast ratio (>= 4.5:1 for text).

rules.name: Diagram Labeling Completeness
rules.description: Every diagram node has a label > 2 chars. Sequence-diagram messages are labeled. Flowchart decision diamonds have explicit yes/no (or equivalent) branch labels.

rules.name: Diagram Embed Completeness
rules.description: Every rendered PNG/SVG in outputs/bid/ has at least one ![]() reference in outputs/bid-sections/*.md. Catches 'PNGs rendered but never embedded' bug where diagrams are absent from evaluator PDFs despite existing on disk.
rules.incident: 2026-05-18: 6 PNG diagrams rendered but ZERO ![]() references in bid markdown; images absent from all PDFs

rules.name: PDF Column Fit and Orientation Check
rules.description: Uses PyMuPDF to check every bid PDF: (1) rightmost text column must not clip within 50pt of page edge, (2) text must not end mid-word at column boundary, (3) orientation must match declared intent (risk register = landscape). Catches 10-column tables on Letter portrait.
rules.incident: 2026-05-18: 10-column risk register PDF on Letter portrait; Owner/Verification/Status columns clipped at right edge

rules.name: Deliverable Table Row Cap Check
rules.description: No _Showing N of M_ row-cap notice in outputs/bid-sections/*.md. No empty mitigation cell (|  |) in risk-priority rows. Same rule as SVA2-DELIVERABLE-NO-ROW-CAPS applied to final bid sections at Gold Team gate.
rules.incident: 2026-05-18: same row-cap and empty-mitigation bugs present in bid-sections that should have been caught earlier at SVA-2

rules.name: Deliverable Table Mid-Word Truncation Check
rules.description: No table cell in outputs/bid-sections/*.md ends with a hyphenated fragment (e.g., 'tamper-') or with a substring match for known truncation signatures ('would manife', 'for entit'). Catches mid-word truncation from upstream extraction artifacts.
rules.incident: 2026-05-18: 'would manife', 'associating those invoices with a repor', 'tamper-' visible in evaluator PDFs
